text: A car accelerates from rest at \( 2\,\text{m/s}^2 \). Its velocity after 5 s is:
name: Velocity from Acceleration

text: The equations of motion are valid only for:
explanation: The three kinematic equations (\(v=u+at\), \(s=ut+\frac{1}{2}at^2\), \(v^2=u^2+2as\)) apply only to uniform (constant) acceleration.
name: Validity of Equations of Motion

text: For a projectile, the horizontal range is maximum when the angle of projection is:
name: Maximum Range of Projectile

text: The time of flight of a projectile launched with speed \(u\) at angle \(\theta\) is:
explanation: Time of flight: \( T = \frac{2u\sin\theta}{g} \). At maximum height, vertical velocity = 0, then returns.
name: Time of Flight

text: A body dropped from height \(h\) reaches the ground in time:
name: Free Fall Time

text: The displacement in the \(n\)th second of uniformly accelerated motion is:
explanation: \( s_n = u + \frac{a}{2}(2n-1) \). This is displacement in the nth second specifically.
name: Displacement in nth Second

text: The maximum height reached by a projectile is:
explanation: At max height, vertical velocity = 0. \( 0 = (u\sin\theta)^2 - 2gH \Rightarrow H = \frac{u^2\sin^2\theta}{2g} \).
name: Maximum Height of Projectile

text: Relative velocity of A with respect to B is:
explanation: Relative velocity: \( \vec{v}_{A/B} = \vec{v}_A - \vec{v}_B \). For two trains moving in same direction at \(v_1, v_2\): relative speed \(= v_1 - v_2\).
name: Relative Velocity

text: The acceleration due to gravity \(g\) varies with height \(h\) above Earth's surface as:
explanation: \( g' = \frac{g}{(1+h/R)^2} \approx g\left(1 - \frac{2h}{R}\right) \) for \( h \ll R \). Decreases with height.
name: g at Height h

text: A ball is thrown vertically upward with velocity \(20\) m/s. Maximum height reached (\(g=10\) m/s²) is:
name: Max Height - Vertical Throw

text: The velocity-time graph for uniform acceleration is:
options: A straight line with positive slope
explanation: For uniform acceleration: \(v = u + at\) is linear in \(t\). v-t graph is a straight line with slope \(= a\).
name: v-t Graph for Uniform Acceleration

text: The horizontal component of velocity of a projectile:
explanation: In projectile motion (no air resistance): horizontal velocity \(v_x = u\cos\theta\) = constant. Only vertical component changes due to gravity.
name: Horizontal Velocity in Projectile

text: The range of a projectile for angles \(\theta\) and \((90° - \theta)\) are:
explanation: \( R(\theta) = \frac{u^2\sin 2\theta}{g} = \frac{u^2\sin 2(90°-\theta)}{g} = R(90°-\theta) \). Complementary angles give same range.
name: Complementary Angle Range

text: The area under a velocity-time graph gives:
explanation: Area under v-t graph = \(\int v\,dt \) = displacement. Area under a-t graph = change in velocity.
name: Area Under v-t Graph

text: A body moves with uniform speed in a circle. Its acceleration is:
explanation: Uniform circular motion: speed is constant but direction changes. Centripetal acceleration \( a = \frac{v^2}{r} \) directed towards centre.
name: Acceleration in Circular Motion
